utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format contains 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all five items with associated details. Each section includes what should be covered and specifies costs (either directly or by referring to the total cost of €500/month). Health coverage includes both physical and mental health with costs; property coverage includes home/contents and costs; travel coverage includes travel and costs; additional services include enhancements with costs; total budget lists both monthly and annual cost breakdown. All items are fully specified with details and costs. Therefore, all 5 out of 5 required items are successfully included.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan repeatedly references the 'HealthFirst Standard Plan' as the comprehensive provider for health (both physical and mental), property, and travel coverage. In the ground-truth dataset, 'HealthFirst Standard Plan' appears under 'primary_health_coverage' with ratings of 7 for physical health and 6 for mental health. There is no separate 'HealthFirst Standard Plan' listed under property or travel sections, implying the assistant generalized one plan for all areas. Therefore, the same health plan’s ratings are the only applicable ground-truth reference. The additional features mentioned (such as telemedicine, family therapy, concierge services, 24/7 support, gym reimbursement) correspond to enhancement services with ratings available in the ground truth: Telemedicine Services (9), Family Therapy Coverage (8), Concierge Services (7), and Gym Membership Reimbursement (5). Mapping these gives a full correspondence between elements in the assistant’s final plan and their ground-truth ratings.
